name: Stephanie's Cinnamon Rolls (Cinnabons)
recipeIngredient: 3 ½ oz package Instant vanilla pudding mix
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the warm water, dry yeast, and sugar. Stir until dissolved. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, prepare the pudding mix and milk according to package directions. Add the margarine, eggs, and salt. Mix well.
3. Gradually add the yeast mixture to the batter and blend together. Gradually add the flour and knead until smooth. Place in a very large greased bowl. Cover and let rise until doubled.
4. Punch down the dough and let it rise again. Roll out on a lightly floured surface to 3 ¼ × 2 inches.
5. Spread 1 cup of melted margarine evenly over the surface. In a small bowl, mix 2 cups brown sugar and 6 tablespoons cinnamon. Sprinkle all over the top of the surface.
6. Roll up from the long end very tightly. Make notches every 2 inches with a knife.
7. Place the rolls on a greased baking pan, 2 inches apart. Press down lightly on each roll with your hand (just a little... it helps hold them together).
8. Cover and let rise until doubled again. Bake at 350°F for 15 to 20 minutes. Remove when they start to turn golden brown. Do not overbake!
9. Frost warm rolls with the following: Cream Cheese Frosting
- 8 oz softened cream cheese
- ½ cup margarine, softened
- 1 tsp vanilla
- 3 cups powdered sugar
- About 1 tablespoon milk (just enough to fluff)
Note: These cinnamon rolls can be made the night before. Mix up the recipe and let it rise once. Punch down the dough and cover for the second rising, placing in the refrigerator overnight to slowly rise. In the morning, complete the rising, roll them out, and finish them up. They also freeze well and can be warmed up nicely in the microwave!
